Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Chinese: 习近平; Pinyin: Xí Jìnpíng) is the current ruler of China and the Chairman of the Communist Party of China (CCP). He was born on 15 June 1953 in Beijing. His father is Xi Zhongxun (Chinese: 习仲勋), who was an old leader of the Communist Party of China.
Xi Jinping’s career began in Fujian province at the start of his tenure, where he carried out political and social work. Later he was appointed as Party leader of Zhejiang province. In 2007, after corruption allegations against Chen Liangyu (Chinese: 陈良宇) in Shanghai, he was appointed as Party chief of an important region.
Xi Jinping is known during his tenure for a strict stance on corruption and for economic reforms. He gave his leadership philosophy for deep reforms in China’s political and economic system. He is called the head of the fifth generation of leadership of the Communist Party of China. In 2012 he was made Chairman of the Communist Party of China, after which he has made significant contributions to China’s political and social development.
Barack Obama:
Barack Hussein Obama (birth: 4 August, 1961) has been the 44th President of America. He is the first Black (African American) President of this country. He took oath of the office of President on 20 January, 2009 and remained in this post until 2017. Obama has been a junior senator from the state of Illinois and in 2008 was the presidential candidate of the Democratic Party.

Obama graduated from Harvard Law School in 1991, where he also was the first African American president of the Harvard Law Review. From 1992 to 2004 he taught constitutional law at the University of Chicago Law. He worked as a civil rights advocate and also worked as a community organizer. In 2000 he failed in obtaining a seat in the American House of Representatives, but in 2004 was elected to the American Senate.

In the 109th Congress as a minority Democrat member he cooperated in the creation of bills on control of conventional weapons and greater public accountability in the use of federal funds. He also made state visits to Eastern Europe, the Middle East and Africa. In the 110th Congress he took part in the creation of bills related to lobbying, election scandals, environmental change, nuclear terrorism and the care of American soldiers returned from war.

For notable contribution in world peace Barack Obama was selected for the 2009 Nobel Peace Prize. His biography is an important chapter in American history, which is an example for the leadership of African American people and national unity.
Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff
Dilma Rousseff was the first female President of Brazil. She was born in 1947 in the city of Espera Feliz in the Minas Gerais state of Brazil. She began her career in the field of education and worked as a teacher. Due to her education and social awareness, she started making her place in politics.
In the 1980s, she increased her presence in politics by joining the Workers' Party (PT). She became known for her leadership for Brazil’s development and social justice. In 2002, she was chosen as the Vice President of Brazil’s President Lula da Silva, and in 2011 she was elected as President. During her presidency, Brazil hosted the 2014 World Cup and the 2016 Olympic Games.
Her government advanced the Bolsa Família program, which is known for fighting poverty and inequality. During her leadership, Brazil achieved many important accomplishments in economic development and social reform.
However, in 2015, allegations of economic crimes were made against her, due to which a charge sheet was presented against her in 2016. She was dismissed during her presidency, which is considered a major event in Brazil’s history. After her dismissal, she was sent to jail, but in 2018 she was released from jail.
